title: [CI:DOCS] Update basic_networking.md
body:
Correct a missing link in basic networking tutorial.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### What this PR does / why we need it:
To correct a missing link in docs/tutorials/basic_networking.md.

#### How to verify it
Just change docs. No need to verify.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *markzhang1996* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@markzhang1996 please sign your commit with `git commit --amend -s` so that CI is happy and we can get this in
LGTM

> @markzhang1996 please sign your commit with `git commit --amend -s` so that CI is happy and we can get this in LGTM

Sorry, I'm not familiar with signing a commit (I never do it before). I see the commit is verified and signed with GitHub’s verified signature because I edit file and commit on GitHub. Doesn't it mean that the commit is already signed?
@umohnani8 It seems like I need a reviewer to type `/lgtm` in comment?

> @umohnani8 It seems like I need a reviewer to type `/lgtm` in comment?

first you need sign your commit.

As @umohnani8 pointed out, you need to run `git commit --amend -s` then push again the change.  I don't think you can use the web UI to do it (unless you can modify the commit message and add a "Signed-off-by: $NAME <$EMAIL>" line to it.

If you did this via the WEB UI, you could just add the following to your commit message

    Signed-off-by:  Mark Zhang <markzhang1994@gmail.com>

If you use git to make this request then, you need to do 

$ git commit -a --amend -s
$ git push --force

@rhatdan It seems like web UI cannot modify comment so I git clone to local and push forcibly.
@giuseppe @rhatdan Many thanks for your explanation and patience.
